objective: Evaluate whether increasing generated component local-move depth from 2 to 3 broadens fresh component-value profile supply, and record an explicit no-go if recursive-node budget pressure collapses usable profiles.
non_negotiables: Do not promote a new clean composition shard from an inventory-only diagnostic.

Do not relax Wave 27 no-reuse leakage gates for positions, decomposition digests, composition digests, decomposition-qualified component identities, component value digests, component value identities, component value pairs, or result value digests.
A learned decomposition-benefit claim remains blocked until a larger leakage-clean split beats deterministic floors and matched no-decomposition controls on the same split.

tasks.prompt: Add a report-only generated-depth-three profile inventory command that reuses the existing generated component patterns and wall-safety rules while evaluating component values at local-move depth 3. Pin the observed support and rejection counts in tests, and prove the Wave 18, Wave 22, and Wave 27 artifacts remain byte-stable.
outputs: Regression test for depth-three accepted profile counts and rejection counts
Stability evidence for Wave 18, Wave 22, and Wave 27 shard hashes and replay reports
acceptance: Accepted profile counts and rejection counts are pinned by a regression test.
If support is worse than depth two, the result is documented as a no-go rather than a scale solution.
Pinned Wave 18, Wave 22, and Wave 27 hashes and replay reports remain stable.

tasks.prompt: Generate the Partizan Wave 34 depth-three inventory report, record its SHA-256, update the experiment matrix, research claims, and verification gates, and keep the evidence boundary clear: this is a report-only no-go diagnostic, not a promoted shard or model result.
outputs: Wave 34 depth-three profile inventory JSON report
Experiment matrix row with artifact hash, source commit, support counts, and no-go boundary
Claim and verification-gate updates preserving the model-readiness blocker
commands: python3 -m py_compile engine/ml_model.py engine/test.py agents/network.py agents/label_schema.py
acceptance: Docs state that depth-three support falls to 6 white and 4 black profiles.
Docs identify recursive-node budget pressure as the main new blocker.
No learned or scale claim is activated from the diagnostic.

tasks.prompt: Use the Wave 34 no-go to constrain the next generator design. The next attempt should not simply increase local-move depth under the same node budget; it must introduce new low-node component geometry, a versioned value rule with explicit split semantics, or a formally documented grouping rule before claiming clean scale.
outputs: Next-wave design constraint in the claim ledger
Verification-gate language preventing repeated depth-increase no-gos
Clear owner handoff to topology_engineer, retrograde_engineer, and dataset_foundry
acceptance: The next-wave recommendation rules out naive depth increase as the scale strategy.
The blocker remains fresh component-value diversity under no-reuse gates.
The claim ledger still blocks learned decomposition-benefit evidence until clean support increases materially.

integration_gates.command: python3 agents/network.py validate && python3 agents/network.py validate-wave agents/waves/wave_34_depth_three_profile_inventory.json
integration_gates.success_signal: The Wave 34 plan is internally consistent.
The report exposes depth-three support counts and rejection reasons.
The committed Wave 34 report is valid JSON.
